nodes.title: Track stars by their numeric value
nodes.why: Each generated star value is unique and stable, so Angular can retain the correct button identity.
nodes.alternative: Track the buttons by an unrelated mutable object or omit a track expression.
nodes.tradeoff: Numeric tracking assumes each value is unique, which holds for the one-through-max range.

nodes.title: Derive the selectable range from max

anchor.snippet: return Array.from({ length: this.max }, (_, i) => i + 1);

nodes.why: The child component renders the configured number of one-based rating choices without hard-coded markup.
nodes.alternative: Write five separate star buttons regardless of max.
nodes.tradeoff: The getter allocates an array, but the API remains reusable for different rating scales.

nodes.title: Emit the accepted rating
nodes.why: The ratingChange output completes Angular two-way binding so the parent currentRating stays synchronized.
nodes.alternative: Update only the child input field and leave the parent value stale.
nodes.tradeoff: The component owns both local state and an event, but consumers get a familiar bindable API.
